If you were to spend more time on it, perhaps.
But for now, staring at this terrible beast full of every secret and whisper you can imagine - you will have to tap into it some other way.
It feels louder than the bowels of this horrendous beast before you.
The beast creeps closer and closer, his smile widening even further.
Your voice echoes through the chamber.
The statement has been said. And it has been heard.
You glance at the goblin giving you a confounded expression.
The aberration shuts its maw and does not say anything as your voice fades.
The goblin looks at you with an expression that mixes confusion with shock.
Of course a celestial would say that.
He curls his tentacles around the edge of the pool.
The telepathic voice of the angel enters your mind. \
The beast lets out a growl.
He gives you a thumbs up.
You've shared this deep part of yourself with the world now.
It feels good. As if you're lighter than you were before.
That's, uh... right, didn't expect that.
It does not matter in the end.
Even if you were not a man of the cloth, it is difficult to find connections in this world.
There is nothing shameful about celibacy.
It's more the involuntary form that's annoying.
You do not have time with dames.
That is not secret. That is a power.
Come on. I need to tell the thing something... deep. Something true.
You do not have it in you.
From your throat to your dong.
.IntelligenceFlashback==1-(Reveal how you found out your esoteric talent.) \
Your goblin companion raises an eyebrow at the comment.
You hear a calming voice enter your mind.
You see the gears start turning in the goblin's mind.
The ancient aberration does not respond as you finish your school days rant.
He's laughing. That goblin is NOT allowed to laugh at you.
Ettir joins him, turning her many, many eyes as to not meet your seeking gaze.
This was a great tale of your origin.
Idiots. All of them- the aberration included.
You had to be there, you know?
Your goblin companion cannot help but show you a shocked, very shocked, expression.
The monster closes his many eyes and growls.
To think another remnant of the Arcanists hides in these tunnels.
You must seek him out, once the election is over.
You could learn much from one of the Arcane Era.
It lets out a grumble and squints the many, many, many eyes.
He opens his maw and clicks his fangs together.
His tentacles snap around the cistern.
He is not interested in debate here.
He's not even interested in your opinion - this guy literally just wants to watch you do whatever it is you're going to do.
The beast knows you are a mythical figure in the making.
An aberration obsessed with fate. This will surely haunt you.
It will watch as you step into LEGEND.
So the monster was in a snail-prison?
It grins with those thousand upon thousands of fangs.
You've read about these stars in some ancient texts. Antesepalian knowledge.
It is speaking truth. Someone's truth.
There must be a catch here. But... what are you going to do about it?
You feel a grain of insight growing in your mind.
You hear the growl coming deeper this time.
You see his grin go wider than ever before.
You mostly just listened, but hey.
For creatures where biology does not determine it, it is entirely an aesthetic determination.
This Trifler is, indeed, very masculine.
Shadowy words appear floating before you.
They read, I, Ragn, swear to uphold the contract of my patron, Ever-Glad.
It is a coward's way out to serve such a monster.
What difference is it from serving a god, really?
This one at least speaks to you...
The shadowy text vaporizes into nothing.
The shadowy text turns red in front of you.
Not exactly what you need right now.
But you learn something from the darkness.
It lets out a deep, echoing growl.
The monster gives you the most shockingly evil smile you've ever seen in your entire life.
If you wish to resist this influence, you will have to be even firmer in your faith.
The world is now a worse place than when you woke up this morning.
Seriously, this is one hell of a deal!
And this guy's clearly not as bad as he looks.
Alas, you can easily break free when the time comes.
Just keep an eye out. You've been through worse deals.
At least this might be a backup gig after the Towers shut down.
You should not mess with this thing.
He's clear about it at least.
You could have broken this overgrown mutated fish.
The only choice now is either to bend, or to leave.
You lift your fist, and look upon the massive maw of the beast.
You must do this. It must be DESTROYED.
You swing your fist right at its scaled hide.
It lets out a series of chortling growls.
The angel has her sword half-drawn.
The goblin is ready to throw himself back and get into sling-position.
You've been doing great work out there!
I'm assuming this is about the asking-people-about-the-election-thing.
About the election! About democracy! About the future!
But no, we're talking about the ELECTION now, buddy. Just like you've been doing quite a lot of lately.
You know what that makes you?
A herald. A Democratic Herald. Doesn't that sound nice?
Oh no. I don't like that at all.
And what exactly does that mean?
I like the sound of that. Democracy!
Can't believe you just said that.
I only asked for people because I am interested in counteracting future democratic movements.
I'm sort of medium on the whole suffrage thing really. Seems like a hassle.
Someone who gets people engaged with the democratic process!
Look, this is what the new world is all about. People talking to each other. Sharing ideas. Debating. Listening. Every conversation is a little spark of democracy igniting.
And you? You're fanning those flames.
So here's the thing: all that talking? It's energizing.
When you engage with people about what matters - when you get them thinking, discussing, caring - it fills you up.
Wait, I don't get any other choices?
Every time you ask someone what they're voting for, you feel a surge of vitality.
It's the excitement of democracy in action!
Democratic Herald - You regain 1d4 hit points each time you ask someone what they are voting in the election.
In this case, you're just way too aware of the fact that you have absolutely no chance of ever subverting/changing/affecting this particular democratic process.
And you obviously can't stop yourself from spreading the word. No matter what that word is.
Though obviously, being a Freestrider, you'll keep this up. Won't you?
You've been doing great work out there!
I'm assuming this is about the asking-people-about-the-election-thing.

So many books. Basic manuals. Arcanist histories. Novels.
Some of them stand out to you as classics. It could be useful to refresh your mind.
He gives you a worried look. \
(Read Autobiography Prax, by Lord Fillius Prax.)
(Read Folk Tales, by The Trill Sisters.)
.ITEMLOOTED_VL_Esoteric_Books_Collected_Chants_of_Norvik==0-(Read Collected Chants of Norvik, by Patriarch Pilli.)
He sits down on some massive tomes and leans against the wall.
You kept this book on your bedside table for years during your scribing-years.
You take a look at the first page.

In these pages, I shall endeavor to recount the journey of a mind born to greatness, a will tempered by the crucible of ambition, and a spirit bound to the relentless pursuit of the Arcane. My name is Fillius Prax.
It goes on to make grandiose statements on his personal abilities of magic.
As always, this man is contradictory. He both praises his own natural gifts and mental abilities, while stating himself worthy only through his hard work and merit.

Know this: I write not for the masses, but for the few who possess the clarity of vision to see beyond the veil of mediocrity. To them, I offer my life as a lesson and a legacy, that they might learn from my triumphs and avoid the pitfalls that lie in wait for the unwary.
You remember the first chapters dealing with his youth, growing up in the ruthless court of a rival house, the Hagginshags.
How he struggles against relentless opposition and bullying.
Making himself out to be an underdog.

Power is not born of blood, nor inherited by the fortune of birthright. It is cultivated in the crucible of the mind, refined by the fires of discipline, and sharpened by the relentless pursuit of knowledge. Let those who seek to rule first learn to kneel before the altar of wisdom, for it is there that true strength is forged.
Yet he does not address his own privilege.
If it is only merit, then he should not have needed any of those fancy private studies or lecturers.
Stop complaining. The story is just getting good.
A glorious design. Let those who learn lead. Through merit of ability and nothing else.
Of course you do. You're a wizard, after all.
The world would be better if I ruled it, like Prax did.
Yet you know the truth all the same.

In the second half, Prax begins to talk about his early political career. The intricacies of magocratic bureaucracy. How he learned to play the 'game' well. Well enough for himself to reach a dictatorship as the youngest magocratic ruler ever.
It's always hard to read a person through pages. But this dude is clearly dancing around his massive <u>ego</u>.
Not that there's anything wrong with that. Impressive even.
The rest is mostly a collection of meditations surrounding magic and power.
A strong leader. With clear intent.

The Esoteric, in its purest form, is neither good nor evil. It is a tool, a force to be wielded by those with the intellect to do so. Yet, let us not be so naive as to think that power alone grants one the right to rule. The Arcanist must also be a statesman, for the whims of unchecked magic can lead even the wisest astray. Govern with an iron hand, but let that hand be guided by the principles of reason and justice. Thus shall the state prosper, and thus shall the legacy of the magocracy endure.
I see not irony here. Only truth.

In the halls of power, the arcane shall be our shield and our sword. Yet, remember this: the arcanist's duty is not only to himself but to the state and its people. For in the endless pursuit of knowledge, one must not forget the price of ignorance. A vigilant mind is the guardian of civilization, and a vigilant state the fortress of progress. Let no shadow fall upon our vigilance, for in the light of our knowledge lies the path to true dominion.
The final parts of the biography focuses on an ever deeper, and more rambling side of ruling.
This is way too much reading.
Why would you open a book then?
It hurts to look at so many words at once.
You shut it, looking upon the title once more.
You place it back on the shelf.
Opening the Trill Sister's book shows you an index of several tales. Many of which you heard growing up.
Stories for children are useless. Do not waste time here.
These stories shape reality. For the mind does not distinguish a tale from a real-life lesson. They are but the same.
Out of the tales, you see the one you remember the most. The one about the gnome and the titan.
This story always spoke to you. Mostly because of the unlikely friendship between the two.
You put it back on the shelf.

In the old days, when dragons ruled the skies and titans wandered the earth, there lived a gnome named Abacus, known far and wide as a halfwit. Whether by fate or folly, he often found himself in the oddest of places, and so it was that he stumbled into a deep valley of Askan.

This valley was home to Kha-Ymor, the Mist Titan, a colossal being with fur as thick as the ancient forests and limbs that clutched the very mountains. His upper body was hidden in, or perhaps made of, the swirling mists that filled the valley. But on this day, the valley was not the serene refuge it had once been. The air was thick with ash, the rivers ran black, and the trees stood as charred husks. Esuth, the ancient red dragon, had set the forests aflame, hoping to drive Kha-Ymor from his domain and claim the fertile valley for himself.

Kha-Ymor was furious, his great form trembling with rage as he searched for the source of the destruction. His eyes fell upon Abacus, who was wandering aimlessly, his tiny mind more puzzled by the sudden darkness than anything else...
To think that the Coast was filled with these Beasts of Jor at one point.
But it's not hard to see why some preferred their chaotic dominance to the tyranny of dragons.
Thrik- lum- chum. Who dares bring fire to where none should come? Ashes taint my sacred mist- speak, small one, lest you be missed.
Abacus looked up, squinting through the fog, and scratched his head.
Fire? No, I've no fire to share. No flame do I bring, nor ash to bear. For in the dark, I see clear as day, 'Tis those who can't, that need the flame, they say!
Thrik- lum- chum. What nonsense from your tongue does come? Who is it that needs this blaze, That blackens my mist and dims my days?
Well question thee, who needs a flame? To light their path and stoke their claim! Perhaps it is Esuth, the king, Whose fires to your valley bring!
Thrik- lum- chum. The dragon's days are surely done! Guide me, gnome, to where he lies, Together, we shall dim his skies!
The story goes on to wax about the friendships sparked between this eldritch titan and the silly little gnome as they sought out Esuth on his throne.
The ancient red dragon then falls to the titan's might, after Abacus accidentally guides him to the correct location.
One of those classic stories every child in Askan is told growing up.
It is important to remember that this is an oral tale passed down over generations.
This is simply the shape in which it was written down. Who knows how exactly it functioned in its original state.
This is a printed edition of Pilli's work. But still old. Probably came from the earliest Norvikian printers.
On the first page, a short message is hand-scribbled in the corner.
My belief lay in the collected humanity of Norvik.
May you remember that, from this collection. With honor and grace, -Pilli D.
Is this... a hand-written message from the Second Patriarch himself?!
You feel faint. Are you even able to hold such an artifact?
Protect this book with your life. This is a sacred artifact.
Fascinating. To think the Pale Man held such a treasure on his shelf.
The book begins with a lengthy essay about the foundation of Norvikian cultural values. It's really like a guide, if anything.
On what a Norvikian person is. How their values come from various 'tribes' in the 'old country', and how together they have formed something greater under Urth.
You've read this before, but you do get a better sense of what this all means now.
It is, in some sense, a plea. A stern suggestion for every human in Norvik to lose any sense of former tribal identity.
To embrace a new one. A Norvikian spirit.
To give away the old and embrace the new.

It is not a completely bad move. To unite a folk is to build commonality. To lower inequality. Although, of course, there were many direct ways the state at the time made sure that the old did not remain in the shadows.
The text directly explains how a new Norvikian culture will help the city grow, and fight against inequity and injustice through unity.
Mainly by being generous and not hoarding wealth, among other things.
Then finally, it does touch upon the connection with dwarves as well, and how the Norvikian spirit is not contained within humans only.
It is not just blood. It is sweat and tears as well. Those folk who unite with us will not be seen as an Other. No, they too are Urth's folk. They too are Urth's guard.
I don't agree with any of this.
Indeed. This is something to live up to.
Lots of old guys in the previous generation spouted fascist rhetoric like this.
I went with you to foreign shores, my lord, my guard.
I pray you keep from harm, the beasts, each shard, a proof of your devotion.
Thus I give myself to your folk.
I give my life for a home at the river. In that sliver of land.
To vanquish the Beasts who roam, the evil who lair, the fires that burn.
To build a life along the river.
Every shepherd knows the song. It bellows up the river. Urth lives in that sliver of land.
Take it. It does not belong in this foul place.
The heroic, powerful man-child wants to steal something?
It is not theft. It is repossession of sacred city-material.
Sure, sure. Let's call it that next time you see a juicy coin pouch.
(Place the book back on the shelf.)
This is a really great book.
DC16 str-Should I really just... take it?
Do not see it as 'take'. You are freeing this sacred object from the pale man's collection.
Yes. That is how you should view it.
You place the book in your pouch.
It'd be hard to sell it here on Tolstad. But you do you.
Sell it? You will protect it.
Even if... taking it from the hold of Pale Man makes you feel conflicted.
You notice a note fall out of the book.
It reads: If you wish to borrow any tome, Cleric, you are allowed.
As you turn around, you see the mortician staring at you.
Then he gives you a thumbs up.
Well. That makes it less exciting.
Why does he simply give it to you?
He might just be a kind-hearted man, below that mask of his.
He is helping you for his own gain. Somehow.
You do so. It fits nicely back into its slot.
This book does not deserve your toxic presence.
If you were stronger... more capable...
It should be yours. Come back later.
One day it could be yours.
This is very confusing. I'm just putting back the book.
Eh, there's some issues with stealing going on here. Just ignore it.
Let us forget this for now.
The book stares at you from the shelf.
So many books. Basic manuals. Arcanist histories. Novels.
Some of them stand out to you as classics. It could be useful to refresh your mind.
He gives you a worried look. \
(Read Autobiography Prax, by Lord Fillius Prax.)
(Read Folk Tales, by The Trill Sisters.)

Guarded by a really riddle-crazed sphinx.
There is no other type of sphinx.
If you count the good ones.
You only saw a tiny piece of the content.
The secrets within were hidden in the darkness. But you saw the light ahead.
...Of Jor? (Think about that big light you saw in the darkness of the Vault.)
Yes. What other Pillars are there?
It was A Pillar, one of the four.
Reaching through the earth, through the Coast.
But it was entirely made out of light.
It is not material in nature. It goes Beyond.
Remember what the gods did - the true gods did - when they arrived in this realm.
They attempted to destroy Jor. For selfish or selfless reasons, no matter which, that is what they did.
But if Jor controls his Gates, it only makes sense from the purely strategic viewpoint that those gods would be desperate to manufacture their own pathways.
The Vault is a Gate to the Beyond?
All truth seems mad until it is proven.
If these gods had their own Gates, then why are they shut?
Why place a sphinx to guard this one? Why place a (relatively speaking) simple magic item inside, as some type of reward?
Maybe someone else took over the Vault, when the outer gods were forced out?
What if Olzis is just fucking around?

Could I have gone deeper? Could the Vault be an actual Gate to worlds Beyond?

If that is the case, and this Gate is used as some type of storage facility, that would be rather silly.

Gods can be pretty silly after all.
There has to be a not-silly explanation to all of this.
Gods are just as flawed as their followers.

Though she will clearly never share the truth with you, if there is another truth to be shared. Not now, at the very least.
The Deck of Many Things is an item with a very high degree of randomness involved with the outcomes.
Whoever placed it there could have done it to play a proverbial prank on the mortals who reach inside. Or it could be a test.
If fate is to be the deciding factor, perhaps fate is the key to finding the 'correct' card in the Deck.
Either way, your life is far too precious to throw away at such a dangerous game.
Do not draw any card at all, if you act in any way as smart as you are.
You did manage to survive an attempt at diving into the deck.
However, if there is a clue inside, you did not find it. Thus, it is not yours to find.

The Strings were all around you. Holding you in place.

It is non-functioning. Looking at it for more than two seconds reveals the reason why: one of the magic runes has been sliced in half.

ROLL14 int-Figure out how to extract the spell.
That is all it takes for you to complete this - to normal men - extremely challenging task.
But you are no normal man.
You are the one who upholds the Arcanist legacy.
You can do this in three hours.
You have not had the pleasure to interact with this spell previously.
But you quickly learn of its extreme usefulness.
Detect Secrets allows you to uncover - once for each cast - any secret trap, hidden passage, stashed away item, or esoteric illusion within thirty feet.
If you wish to uncover the secrets of the City Below, make sure to keep it prepared.
It must be quite new, however, as you have not heard of it being used by adventurers, nor by any clerics during any of the official delves.
It vibrates in your hand as you utter the keyword.
Then, as if snapping to attention, an esoteric force pushing it to point- straight upwards.
For about three seconds it holds this perfectly vertical alignment, until it falls back down into your grasp, limp.
Moving just a few steps to the side whilst the pickaxe is erect shows it clearly.
It is pointing ever-so slightly - in a faint lean - towards...
A few more steps reveals that to actually be the case.
That is interesting. The green one found useful information for once.
This is nothing like your regular memorization.
A spell scroll or codex is often nothing more than taking the original content and transcribing it- putting the various esoteric stages to memory in the process.
A magic item however is crafted to only output those stages per automata, not giving any insight to the inner workings.
It is, by definition, reverse engineering.
Oh. Then I should just be able to retrace the steps of the pickaxe's esoteric activation - which Strings are being pulled and when - writing them down step by step.
Yes, normally that could work. Even if it is a highly difficult and slow process.
However, this pickaxe is broken. It does not perform those esoteric stages in proper order, or more likely, one of the stages is broken.
Entirely due to that last rune being sliced.
It will, no matter what you do, take around three hours for you to do this.
Make sure you have the time before you commit.
You do not figure it out.
Not now, at least. But even if you do manage to understand the mechanics at play here, you would still be forced to invest several hours to perform the extraction itself.
All due to the broken nature of that final rune.

Primarily for the sake of gaining love and admiration and respect and all that good shit.

The kobold looks at you with a cold expression. Determined. Confident.
He is not glaring at you. That is just his default expression.
The kobold appears proud. There is no humility in its voice.
A strong little one. Nearly immovable.
Oh fuck. Yeah, that's not a question. Quick, save it!
ROLL10 dex-Try to save the question.
Think of a less explosive question.
He gives you an expression that could be described as a frown. That is, if it looked in any way different from his regular stony expression.
Must be very well for such fierce devotion.
The pressure of keeping what meager food and shelter you can scrape together with your salary is no different from serfdom. Mourn this pitiful creature. MOURN.
He gives you an expression that could be described as a frown. That is, if it looked in any way different from his regular expression.
Calling something 'super legal' makes it sound very not.
I think he's threatening you, but it's hard to pick up over how adorable he is.
The goblin leans over to you. \

Yhelgen's Accord -- A signed agreement between districts in Norvik allowing the free use of arms and magic below a 'reasonable amount'. It would allow a freelance mercenary the access to a crossbow, for example.
It's not legal for you to steal a crossbow. For some reason. I think.
That question is why the Yhelgen's Accord is a complete disaster.
Let's put it like this: An army of a hundred armed orc mercenaries could be considered 'reasonable', if enough bribes were handed out.
A man should be allowed to defend himself. It is only just.
Plus, it makes fighting evil citizens a lot more exciting.
You would make greater use of it than this puny creature. Just take it.
For whatever reason he must have been ordered not to hurt you. Don't push it.
Don't let this slide. Just take the crossbow!
That wasn't a question. It was a demand!
The kobold stares at you for a moment.
A rhetorical question. So, yeah, the kobold is technically correct.
Snell gives you a stern look.
The goblin doesn't want to become an accomplice. Weak! Maybe when he's not around...
The kobold makes a cackling noise. \
The kobold makes a growling noise.
This kobold just keeps getting better and better.
Kobolds don't even drink milk. I want to know *more*.
The goblin is holding in laughter.
I want to know a lot less.
Don't ask questions about the milk.
The kobold stares at you. With. Very. Yellow. Eyes. Yeah.
The kobold makes a low rumbling noise.
The spirit of this little kobold...
He even managed to rope you into his little scam. How cute.
You can't keep up with this...? This is crazy?
Who buys MILK from a CLERIC?
Eh. You could probably manage one bottle at least.
The expressionless cold-blooded kobold is hard to read, but his body language does not exactly scream honesty.
Side hustles are always smart. You should do a milk run later!
They are draconic in nature. They lay eggs.
Milk is a form of pure vitality. Nature's most precious liquid.
You've acclimatized your gut to the unnatural consumption of another species' milk. Like an enormous babe clinging to its mother's teat.
The kobold doesn't seem to crave the white liquid, despite your comment.
You smirk. A confident laugh escaping your lips. You then turn towards the ceiling, smiling.
Your comment has made a dent upon the world.
What a stance! As a human, who could have ever thought such a thing about you!
This is why they should all follow you. You are a worthy leader.
Win them over with lies and deceit. Control them through flattery and love.
The kobold stares at you, with awe in his yellow expressionless eyes.
You start stuttering. Sweating. A piece of your soul leaves your body as the words escape your lips.
No one in the universe believes you.
The kobold stares at you, with disbelief in his yellow eyes.
This knowledge will be very useful.
You should totally find out more about this.
You probably shouldn't look too deeply into this.
There's probably no money to be made in milk. Right?
Shocking, perhaps. Although it takes a special kind of creature to vote in some kind of pragmatic selflessness.
This is a good kobold. Very good kobold.
He has PRINCIPLES. Tell him he is a good boy.
Snell glares at you after your comment.
The goblin's face seems to indicate a certain level of wincing.
The taste of blood fills your mouth. The pain changes your brain chemistry for a moment, leaving your mind in shambles.
Oh no! Hurry, save the situation - say something! Anything!
You can't speak in this condition. Don't even try.
Use this to your advantage. Intimidate him with your life's liquid.
Blood starts to drip down your chin.
(Spit out blood through the gap in your helmet.)
Try to tell him that you've injured yourself.
As you open your mouth a stream of fresh blood comes pouring down the bottom of your helmet.
He is baffled. Your actions have truly made a mark upon his dark soul.
You've somehow managed to make the stoic kobold speechless. Congratulations.
Your inexplicably perceivable bloody smile is sure to cause havoc.
You wipe the remaining blood off.
There's more where that came from. To a certain degree.
There's a limited supply of that. Just so you know.
Oh, uh. How do we do this...? Appeal to his better nature? I don't fucking know.
Stealing would be evil. So just demand it! Loudly!
ROLL15 cha-Find a reasonable argument. (Probably with a lie.)
A strange noise escapes the kobold's maw.
Oh! You know exactly what to say...
There's only one thing that could possibly convince this kobold that you should get the crossbow.
The kobold glances over to his master.
The kobold seems to believe you. Somehow.
The kobold reluctantly and slowly hands over his crossbow.
The fact that he handed it over like that... the pale man sure is special.
With the crossbow safely in your grasp, the kobold scoffs. \
He leans back and smirks. Then nods.
The fact that he handed it over without even asking the pale man first... love it.
This is difficult. The kobold already hates your guts. Don't make it worse.
That's true, you're a slow learner.
You can see a hint of fear in his eyes as he glances back and forth between you and his master.
That would be a pretty dumb law, to be fair.
Great restraint. Impressive. His reptilian expression reveals little, but his tone says 'I'd kill you if I could.'
Well that was a failure. Better find some other way of getting the crossbow.
You see the kobold stare at you as you approach.
Just go for it! The crossbow isn't even loaded.
The kobold greets you with an enthusiastic tone as his eyes clearly show a veil of charm.
He almost shoves it into your hands.
Charms are the best. Makes everyone love you, just the way you deserve.
There is something truly foul about using magic to gain property.
A dark shock overwhelms the tiny kobold.
Yeah. Visken has this guy under a tight leash, doesn't he?
Glancing over to the pale man, you find him working away without a care in the world.
The kobold suddenly lets out a deep growl.
You can see the charm lose its power over him, as the faint veil is lifted over his eyes.
He takes a few steps back.
The held-back rage within this kobold... it's an inferno.
You've angered the kobold. Not that it really matters, unless you're planning on persuading him in the future.
You look down at the crossbow with the sort of glance that can only say one thing.
The kobold is ready as you reach forward.
Yes! Defeat the little green one!
You can do this! You're going first!
Oh shit, you're not fast enough. Damn it.
The kobold attempts to scratch your hands!
He gets a nasty hit in!
Your turn to act! What will you do?
Destabilize the kobold. Kick his box.
Great idea! You just gotta be quick enough.
Ask him nicely to let go.
Put all your might into a final finisher! You can do this!
If you do that, you might just fuck yourself over.
Why won't he just let me have it?
ROLL12 str-Push yourself to the limit - tighten your grip of the crossbow!
ROLL11 cha-Ask him nicely to let go.
ROLL8 dex-Destabilize the box he's sitting on.
He probably doesn't like you enough. Work harder!
You strain your muscles to the limit.
You strain your muscles, but you can't seem to get a better grip.
The kobold starts shaking the crossbow violently from left to right.
The kobold holds even tighter than you. Damn.
He seems to enjoy the ideas you're inserting into his mind.
You see the kobold's eyes light up in rage.
He's not actually changing his expression, but you can feel the fires.
Holy shit, you really know how to make a little green guy mad.
You attempt a swift kick forward, managing to hit the crate.
It moved! A little bit at least.
The kobold appears to have been disrupted by this action.
His grip of the crossbow appears to have weakened. Somewhat.
You line up the kick and swing your boot forward.
But you lose your footing! Damn!
You end up swinging into thin air.
The tension rises. Blood pumping. Be ready.
Even angrier this time, the kobold attempts to snap at you with his maw.
He chomps down on your gloved hand. A shooting pain goes up your arm.
It aches. You need to hold out.
Don't just brute-force it. Actually think for once.
ROLL12 con-Hold out. Make it an endurance contest.
ROLL7 int-Come up with an actual plan.
Increasing pressure ever so slightly for a few seconds, you eventually see the strain on the kobold's face.
He cannot keep up with your iron grip.
Then you feel your body failing your orders.
You wrack your mind for solutions.
And one little tidbit comes up: a long time ago you heard a fact about kobolds.
Apparently if you get close enough to one, you can blow on their faces to make them terribly uncomfortable.
Sure, let's try it. (Lean in over the crossbow.)
The kobold makes a loud growling noise as you lean in.
In a violent burst, the kobold sneezes.
He almost loses his grip of the crossbow in the process. Well done.
Don't worry. I've got an idea.
(Just scream for a while. That should work.)
You forcefully press your helmet against the top of the crossbow, realizing too late that you cannot physically lick the surface due to the iron plate.
You lift your head to the sight of a very confused kobold, still struggling against you.
What was your plan here, exactly?
Figured he wouldn't want it if I licked it.
Ah. Smart of you. It would have been a masterful plan if the helmet didn't get in the way.
As the thought lingers in your mind for longer than a few seconds, you feel like a deranged fool.
You seem to have put an idea in the kobold's little mind.
Your screeching voice echoes through the chamber.
Your weak lungs making it sound like a little baby bird longing for its mother.
Well done. You've made him madder.
The kobold attempts to grab something at his side.
You realize now where the crossbow is aimed.
Ôst reaches forward with incredible dexterity.
Your intense willpower and body-cohesion protects you from the worst of the damage.
But somehow you open your eyes.
Sent straight into your gut, the bolt got caught in your chain mail.
This is your final chance. You can feel it.
The kobold is making a move... do something!
The kobold is still in shock over your... surprising recovery. Make a move!
ROLL12 str-Make your final attempt. Rip the crossbow out of his grip!
Can't he and I just... talk this out?
Impossible at this point. He's too determined to let go without the use of force.
There were of course other paths than violence that could have been taken.
Do not cry over broken milk, green one.
It is now only time to impose our manhood over this weak creature.
The kobold is... not in the mood for talking. You're certain of that.
Yeah, talk to him. It'll be fine!
The pocket watch. Bring it here.
ROLL30 cha-Convince him to give up.
...and a deep love for crossbows - you rip the glorious artifact out of its owner's little claws.
With the crossbow safely in your grasp, the kobold gives you an intense glare, panting.
He reaches for his sidearm. An adamantine dagger.
He glances to his side, towards his master. He glances back to you.
You watch as the kobold holds in his rage. An incredible feat, after what you did.
Weak little kobold. Perhaps you should give it back, once you're done with your glorious quest.
You see the kobold's eyes get covered in the veil of charm.
Mind control is such an abhorrent type of magic.
The air tastes like failure as your grip of the crossbow loosens.
The kobold snorts and clutches his precious weapon.
You do that. You, you get up, you get up from your failed state.
The kobold looks at you smugly with an expressionless expression.
You feel the world distort around you.
You're experiencing an esoteric event. Stay calm, and you might survive this.
You take a deep breath. A calm washes over you in this strange situation.
A series of shallow breaths causes your heart to beat faster and faster.
Strings. Strings attached to every living being.
To every soul. There... there's the kobold's!
You cannot know. This is not a place for logic.
Only feel. That is all you can.
That is a decision. You cannot make those here.
You cannot know. You simply must follow the flow.
At least you have your pocket watch.
You follow them as they dance.
To side, to forth. To back. To now.
You see three Strings in front of you. See?
The String where you see yourself defeated, but also without any wounds.
The String where you see yourself victorious, but heavily wounded.
The String with a smooth talking Cleric about to be handed a crossbow.
No. You cannot grab it. It simply... slips away.
I'm going to die?! Here? Now?
But you will land on a quite boring String.
A great idea pops into your skull.
It's time to... propose a trade!
The uniform is too important. Plus you can't walk around naked. That'd be obscene.
What are you going to offer him, a rotting tooth?
This kobold already lives with this pale wizard guy. He's got a ton of magic items, surely.
I'm sorry to say, this crossbow goblin is much wealthier than you.
Why do you need crowns? You're rich.
If you think you can make it past the red guy. So... no.
Not that tempting of an offer, is it?
More of a burden if anything.
I am! I'm great at massages.
When did you ever massage anyone?
True. It's more of a metaphorical massage.
I don't think the kobold likes those.
Are you still holding the crossbow?
The kobold stares at you with a glowing intensity.
How the hell did you think you'd sneak the crossbow away from him? Come on!
Tell him you were stretching or something.
Just smile. No one can be mad at a smile like yours.
You'll just look insane if you do that.
Relax. Let's get you out of this.
The crossbow is now pointed directly towards your chest.
Good job, you've pissed off the kobold.
Going from annoyed to pissed off doesn't really matter.
Whoops. Now you gotta get the crossbow some other way.
But you do have to get the crossbow.
Actually, you don't. Stealing is immoral.
Who would have imagined such a thing.
The beatings of life upon the shores. Each wave another painful twinge.
The heart laid square. Blood in veins. Fear in glands. Peace upon the earth.
I have the key. Now I'm ready to dive in.
The smoking man must be stopped. He is the one behind it all.
One cog. A machine, like the Rollermill.
His actions were begun by another, who was begun by another, who was begun by another...
The smoking man. In the PILLAR.
No, I heard. I just don't understand anything. At all.

.QP8_Gate==1-I'll make use of Ever-Glad's pass. That'll work great.
The spellman. His pale face twisted into nothing.
A creature of pure evil, with nothing but INTRIGUE as guide.
A creature of ancient breath, its wisdom wrought in divine slop.
Grand vision. Little love. Drunk on life and its promises.
She is not a force of good. No longer.
Now, she is but a spirit for barrels and drink.
The saboteur. Him in the Pipes.
It is always close. An ear held up to the city's toiletry- its secret whispers of flatulence and despair.
You see him as he smiles. He put this in motion, and changed his tracks along the way.
You surprised him. Now, you serve him.
The path forward lay clear nonetheless.
As your journey comes to a close.

.SmarterIntro==1-It's Smarter. He's just the type to get into trouble...

You do not know anything. There is no clear proof.
You need fact. You need tangibles.
That was just a random guess, so yeah.
Darn. I only get one guess?
An arrow in the dark. However...
You think as much as you will. However...
If you wish to know the truth...
It shall calm your rumbling stomach. Once and for the end of time.
In that, you shall find the Dark Belly of the Soul.
That which hides within. And spews without...
As will you, the heart. That beating thing which dreams of a world without strife.
One of love and community. Of friends. One friend. Dear, oh dear, Hjârtat Mitt.

Is it so simple of a question?
That thought that causes your muscles to tense up and cramp.
The question you've been asking yourself since you were five years old.
And does it seem they are all dead and buried, long before your time?
Tell me more about Dark Belly of the Soul.
Tell me more about A Good Man.
I'm ready to make a decision. (Choose a feat.)
Facing the end, to see the light.
Dark Belly of the Soul - Resistance to all damage when below 50% HP.
Hold everyone and everything in your heart. If there is no space, fix it or die.
Hjârtat Mitt - Each level of exhaustion grants +1 to each d20 roll instead of -1.
It drives you. It fuels you.
The dream of becoming exactly what you were meant to be.
A Good Man - Whenever you survive a death saving throw, you get back up with 1d20 HP.
The rumbling will stop, only once the ending comes.
Survive until then. You will. Do it.
There is love for all in your chest.
But with this, you know: one step has been taken.
A step in the very much RIGHT direction.

Messages from lost people and their culture?
But more importantly, it could have a HOARD of CROWNS!

Some of them drop to the ground and you pick them up.
You wave your arm around inside, until something... STICKS you.
As you rip it out of the bag, a sharp pain runs across your palm.
Something cut you inside. Something sharp.
As you do, you realize there is a faint trace of dust along the edge.
Saying those words causes a canister to plop out of the ether.
With some finesse you feel around the rim of the void within.
You feel around the rim, but...
When you do, something sticks to your hand!
You do so, causing your hand to snap back out of the bag- hitting yourself in the face.
Usually languages and words lie within the blue one's expertise, but this is not so much a 'knowing' as much as it is an intuition.

Even if money is not everything, it is quite lovely to have it- to give to those less fortunate.

The last thing you hear is Snell saying something about 'health and safety'.
Then you shove your head into the bag.
Yeah, I am pretty hungry actually.

A wave of apples smash against your face in the void.
(Plop your head out from the bag.)

The true order was corrupted and betrayed long ago.
During the Arcane Era it was men who rose above all else. Wizards, with unimaginable power used a perfect system of meritocracy to lift themselves up and build empires.

Azgal was a simple underground dwarven male, born during the Divine Era. He was a dwarf who came from nothing but resentment and anger.
It is not clear whether or not he was truly a demi-god. But in his death, he had left a ruined empire in his wake- millions starving. A thousand-year legacy of dwarven history- destroyed.
He wanted to take the amazing riches gathered by the many dynasties of the Lower Empire and simply hand them out to the population.
An idealistic revolutionary. Some see him as precursor to the Freestrider Movement. But in comparison, miraculously, the Freestriders look somewhat competent.
The dwarves beneath Askanii never recovered from Azgal. Today his followers are known as Exiles, since they are not allowed to even mention his foul name underground.
Yet here in our city we allow Azgalists to gather and share their toxic ideal freely?
We are lucky they do not have the same kind of backing the Freestriders have. That would be the end of everything you hold dear.
This Azgal fellow sounds like my kind of dwarf.
Then perhaps you are more lost than I originally thought.
It is the truth. Nothing but the truth.
It would certainly be cowardly to not make a decision at all, and might even anger many. Although being 'apolitical' would certainly allow you to circumvent many disrupting situations.
Truly, it is up to you.
Though remember, it is as intellectually dishonest to take a position of neutrality due to fear of saying the wrong thing as it is to take a position for your own gain.
A true genius speaks true. Listens. Then speaks again.
You have already made it clear. Many times.
Come on. It's the new world!
The Freestriders will make you - and every one around you - RICH!
Social democracy is the only ideology that does not trample upon the safety of the working person.
Azgalism, as it appears in Norvik, is good. True good.
It will not result in a win. However, it is a statement of intent.
Your stomach rumbles. The Agrarians are not even represented.
This is very upsetting to your gut.
No one will ever know what you vote for. So it really, really does not matter.
Though you might be able to control people better if you really internalize how devoid you are of any political thoughts. So...
I'm ready to make a decision. (Choose a feat.)
Nationalist - Extra proficiency in rolls when speaking with humans.
Freestrider - Gain a 10% additional discount in all purchases.
For your brothers and your sisters. For everyone below you.
To flatten this uneven city. To make everything just. And good.

Azgalist - Whenever you take damage, move one step forward on a secret three step counter. On step one, a 'thesis' d20 die is thrown. On step two, if the damage you took is higher than the 'thesis' die, a 'antithesis' d4 die is thrown. On step three, the damage you took is lowered either by 1, or by the 'antithesis' die if you rolled one during step two. This is 'synthesis', somehow. The next time you take damage, return to step one.
Essentially, you must perform a three step process of crafting, meeting, and unity.
It is the good choice for Norvik.
The greatest wizard of the coast.
Arcanist - When you use up your last spell slot of any level, you roll a 1d100. If the result is lower than your natural intelligence, you regain that spell slot.
Agrarian - Whenever you pick up food, you roll 1d20. If you get 16 or higher, you get an additional food item.
Objectively smart. Be whatever you need to be - to win.
Apolitical - Increases proficiency in rolls by 1. This amount is increased to 10 on any election day.
You will make the world better.
Oh yes, you are! Very neutrally so.
You feel a great deal of knowledge from dealing with this burning question in your heart.
You have expressed some opinions lately.
There is nothing within it that interests you intellectually.
Except, of course, the prospect of yourself upon that vacant throne.
Now you will listen to true reason.
You see, what all this has been, is an opportunity.
One to share this tumultuous part of yourself. With others.
So now that part lives out there. It will stay there for some time, collectivizing into the zeitgeist. Growing. Decaying. Until it festers into an unimaginable beast of bureaucracy.
That, in its nature, is an election. A general election of leadership and governance.
It is, of course, a complete farce.
Why do I feel the need to ask strangers about their voting choices all the time?
I know what to vote for. (Make a decision.)
ROLL15 int-Attempt to ask yourself, for real this time: what should I vote in the election?
Because no one seeks answers. Only power.
The true order was corrupted and betrayed long ago.
During the Arcane Era it was men who rose above all else. Wizards, with unimaginable power used a perfect system of meritocracy to lift themselves up and build empires.
These great men fell when the rules betrayed them. Their powers stifled, their minds corroded. The Coast War was bloody, and sealed the fate of this order.
Great men? What about great women?
I really hope we can return to the great order.
They deserved to fall. They were tyrants.
It is not as simple as that. There are many reasons why men rule among humans. Just like there are many reasons why humans rose above all others during the Arcane Era.
One day you will tackle this topic thoroughly. With statistics. It is not this day.
Do not hope for order. You must simply strive towards it each day with a tranquil and open mind.
Tyrants, you say? No. If you look upon history with meek eyes, you will see: these men could have been tyrants. All of them: philosopher kings.
Keep asking. Eventually their weak facade will fall off. The truth being only a disappointing allegory for mortality.
The search for agency. For meaning.
What you do, is seek support.
Your brothers and sisters have different thoughts than yourself. You wish to mingle yours with theirs.
You ask to tell. To share the knowledge: Norvik is for the Urthfolk.
Humans must come first. This is your city.
Tsk. You ask for lots of different reason. Number one being: because democracy is exciting!
Asking such a question is not valid.
If you need to ask, then you are not ready to learn.
But do not regret your inquiry.
But seriously, what should I vote for? (Ask your intelligence )
Being blunt is healthy. As such, you should listen to the following well:
There is no good choice here.
I will vote for the Nationalists then.
I could vote for the Freestriders?
Fuck it, what about the god damn Azgalists?
So I shouldn't vote at all?
Fine. Then I'm ready. (Make a decision.)

Or rather, it might as well not. You know of old man Prax and his gathering of so-called wizards. However, branding a political party around the abhorred ideology of the previous era is not an intelligent idea.
Add to that old man Prax's imbecilic behavior, and you've got a failed party for idiots.
The Nationalist Party of Norvik is a stale relic of theocracy and bureaucratic fascism.
Ever since their demi-god URTH died, they have been about as efficient as a dwarf on a bicycle. Their city decaying so much in these decades, they willingly organized an election.
It is as if they subconsciously understand their decadence - crafting exactly what they deserve.
Like a weak man who smiles as his wife crushes his <u>ego</u>. Day after day.
Giving up power is a sign of competence.
The Nationalists are <shake>RUINING</shake> this fine city.
They should just destroy the other parties and reclaim their great past!
Yes, you are correct. If that choice is based on knowledge - and, most importantly, if it is an actual choice.
Giving up the illusion of power because you have none in reality is only a sign of incompetence. Nothing else.
They won't put me in charge.
There is more to it than that. But yes.
If Urth returned tomorrow, this would clearly be the outcome.
Yet Urth is long dead, along with the legends of the past.
And the future is clearly female, or whatever these liberals say.
And allow the opportunists of New Diminor strangle this ancient city even more so?
They already own most of it. Giving them de jure power would not change much, I agree.
But it doesn't matter how much they promise to keep Norvik independent -- if they win they will surely incorporate it into their Free-City agreement.
At first through legal loopholes. Then through the guise of increasing wealth. Then by simple lack of animosity among the populace.
The Freestrider Revolution is a poison. It is already in your veins.
If a dozen dwarves knocked on your door and asked you to hand over all your wealth with the promise that it would be returned when you really needed it -- would you trust them?
What if they took just a little bit?
That is how they get you. You see
You are not the first to say that.
They would never stop there. You see
Azgal was a simple underground dwarven male, born during the Divine Era. He was a dwarf who came from nothing but resentment and anger.
It is not clear whether or not he was truly a demi-god. But in his death, he had left a ruined empire in his wake- millions starving. A thousand-year legacy of dwarven history- destroyed.
He wanted to take the amazing riches gathered by the many dynasties of the Lower Empire and simply hand them out to the population.
An idealistic revolutionary. Some see him as precursor to the Freestrider Movement. But in comparison, miraculously, the Freestriders look somewhat competent.
The dwarves beneath Askanii never recovered from Azgal. Today his followers are known as Exiles, since they are not allowed to even mention his foul name underground.

You will make the world better.
Oh yes, you are! Very neutrally so.
You feel a great deal of knowledge from dealing with this burning question in your heart.
The knowledge of lacking. Of being without, this thing hanging veil-like as a connective tissue between others.
Is it something? Or is it simply void?
Why are you asking such a weird question?
What's this got to do with the tea shop?
Sounds like a mathematical thought for the blue one.
Everything. It is the central theme.
You come here to solve a mystery.
Yet what you find is everything working against you.
Like, why didn't I get access to the tea shop straight away?
Someone must really not like me.
Only your presence here is due to a grudge. Not the difficulty of this quest.
Political forces working against each others. Not in direct competition, but indirectly- as the election looms over all folk.
A Coinlord who wishes nothing to brought to light under his reign.
A Chieftain who wishes to hold her domain tightly.
Caught between: a poor woman in a tea shop.
How do you know she's innocent?
I'm well on my way to dealing with this already. Just gotta keep going a bit further.
...And what's this got to do with loneliness?
Not innocent. But somehow you know she did not deserve such an end.
For you, despite these tribulations, it has proven a revitalization.
This last year has been terrible.
It was just a demotion. I've been through worse.
You are on your way to handle it all.
You have had those for many years.
But it is your new companion who has been the turning point.
His presence is a good in your life.
Nor is beauty. Nor is love.
They do not help you survive this world.
Rather, each of those things give value to survival.
So the empty hole in your chest does not open up as wide at the end of your next quest.
When the silence of your tower office or room at mom's apartment does not engulf you quite so suddenly and hastily.
But then you can ask yourself another question.
A warm liquid, filtered through leaves of various distinguished plants.
Only to be served to free-spirited youthful folk of common cloth.
A drink of the Post-War Era.
Some of them are lost, yes. But that does not a fool make.
You see, at the center of Norvik, in the middle of the chilled River Torna, sits a fortress.
One filled with folk. Each of them flowing through the alleys and tunnels and streets.
At the center of which, you find a tea shop.
Stepping through its doors you're immediately hit by a wave of exotic aromas.

There are folk of all kinds debating, playing music, and exchanging ideas within these walls. Stacks of newspapers thrown about the tables. Behind the counter you see a dark-feathered creature serving freshly brewed cups.
This is a TEA SHOP. There are many like it in Norvik. There is nothing special about this one, apart from the fact that this one blew up.
Wait, what's the tea shop called, again?
I used to love tea shops.
Since it is now left only a memory to those who loved its comfort.
It was the best years of your life.
It was the closest you ever came to having real friends.
Most of it spent drinking halfling-tea in Dvârgby.
Aren't you the one always going on about exploited labor? Well that's you.
A lonely kid, tricked into pushing dwarf propaganda. That's all.
It is not so simple. They were also kids. Myran, she was trying to-
Nothing to cry over. Nothing lost.
Everything gained however. If you hadn't gotten arrested, you never would have joined the Urthguard.
It was destiny. A group of Azgalists turning on each other, running to the hills- to survive that? It builds character.
Keep this goblin at arm's length.
You don't know where he's been, or what his intentions are. You will make it as a Lone Cleric against the world. As always.
No, no. Don't push him away.
You gotta reel him in! Just like everyone else.
Your main problem from the beginning has been one thing: honesty.
You keep showing your true colors. Nobody likes that.
Keep it friendly. Keep it light.
They can enjoy your company without it being a winning game.
But if they enjoy your company, you win.
Focus on your spells. Learning, retaining knowledge, and applying it during your downtime.
Become an even greater Master of Memorization.
Okay, I'm ready to make a choice. (Pick one of the feats.)
You're a man. You don't need friends.
Taking a short rest means kneeling down in the dirt, a fist placed upon your chest, your brow dripping, pearls of sweat falling down upon your bulging pecs, in the dark, alone, by yourself.
You don't need friends. You're a man.
Lone Cleric - At the start of your Short Rest, heal an additional amount equal to the levels of exhaustion you have.
Normally, your absolute genius allows you to recover a spell slot during whatever breathers you take.
Focus on this. The rest is useless.
Master Memorizer - During your Short Rest, you restore two random spell slots instead of one.
Snell is a good person. You enjoy his company. Just his presence during a breather is enough to make you feel good at heart.
You seriously need to learn how to make friends. For real.
That includes manipulating them into actually sticking around.
Friendly Company - During Short Rests, always roll Hit Die with advantage. Snell also gains an extra proficiency in his initiative rolls.
So is this a... wise or charismatic thing to choose?
Like with alignment, it is all about intention.
Do you view socialization as a zero-sum game?
Which is the correct way of seeing it.
A relationship is what you make it.
If you assume it to be a cold calculation, then that is what you get.
Go with your heart. It lives in your sleeve.
An intelligent choice, from a master wizard.
